description:
You are helping a climate-science researcher clean up and structure several raw data files that were downloaded to the workstation.

INITIAL STATE (already present when the terminal session starts)
1. Directory structure
   • /home/user/downloads/          ← current “drop-zone” for downloaded files  
   • /home/user/research/           ← general research area (empty except for the directory itself)

2. Files inside /home/user/downloads/  
   • climate_temp_v1.txt        (see exact contents below)  
   • climate_temp_v2.txt        (see exact contents below)  
   • climate_precip_v1.txt      (see exact contents below)  
   • readme.txt                 (a completely unrelated file that must remain in /home/user/downloads/)

3. Exact contents of the three raw data files  
   /home/user/downloads/climate_temp_v1.txt  
        date,temp  
        2024-01-01,23  
        2024-01-02,22  

   /home/user/downloads/climate_temp_v2.txt  
        date,temp  
        2024-01-01,24  
        2024-01-02,23  
        2024-01-03,25  

   /home/user/downloads/climate_precip_v1.txt  
        date,precip_mm  
        2024-01-01,5  
        2024-01-02,0  

WHAT THE RESEARCHER NEEDS (the desired final state)
A. A new folder hierarchy under /home/user/research/datasets/ with the following structure:
   └── climate/
       ├── raw/        ← will hold the renamed *.csv files
       └── doc/        ← reserved for documentation (empty for now)

B. The three *.txt data files must be:
   1. Moved from /home/user/downloads/ into /home/user/research/datasets/climate/raw/
   2. Renamed so their extension becomes .csv, keeping the base file names identical.
      Resulting file names inside raw/ must therefore be:
         • climate_temp_v1.csv
         • climate_temp_v2.csv
         • climate_precip_v1.csv
      Their internal contents must **not** be modified.

C. Symbolic link
   • /home/user/research/datasets/climate/latest.csv must be a **symbolic link** that points to  
     /home/user/research/datasets/climate/raw/climate_temp_v2.csv  
     (because v2 is the most recent temperature file).

D. Summary file
   • Create /home/user/research/datasets/climate/summary.txt  
   • Each line must have exactly two comma-separated fields in this exact format:
         filename,line_count
     where:
         – filename is just the file’s basename (e.g., climate_temp_v1.csv)  
         – line_count is the total number of lines in that CSV file (including its header)  
     With the given datasets, the file must contain **precisely** these three lines, in any order:
         climate_temp_v1.csv,3  
         climate_temp_v2.csv,4  
         climate_precip_v1.csv,3  

E. Operation log  
   • Create /home/user/research/datasets/operations.log  
   • It must contain exactly six lines, numbered 1-6, documenting each major step in order.  
   • Use the following fixed wording (copy it verbatim, do not add timestamps):
        1. Created directory tree
        2. Moved and renamed files
        3. Verified file contents
        4. Created symbolic link
        5. Generated summary.txt
        6. Cleanup complete

CONSTRAINTS
• Do not touch /home/user/downloads/readme.txt.  
• Only standard GNU/Linux user-level tools may be used (no sudo).  
• Keep permissions at their defaults (the grader only checks existence, names, contents and symlink target).  
• All path names are case-sensitive; spell them exactly as specified.

VERIFICATION NOTES (what automated tests will check)
1. The directory /home/user/research/datasets/climate/raw/ exists.  
2. The three .csv files exist there, each with byte-for-byte identical contents to the source .txt files.  
3. /home/user/research/datasets/climate/latest.csv is a symlink and readlink resolves to raw/climate_temp_v2.csv.  
4. /home/user/research/datasets/climate/summary.txt is present and its lines are exactly the three specified above (order insensitive, but no extra whitespace).  
5. /home/user/research/datasets/operations.log exists, contains the six numbered lines exactly as written.  
6. /home/user/downloads/readme.txt is still present and unchanged.

Using roughly 5-10 shell commands is sufficient to complete everything.
truth:
Initial files (already on filesystem before agent starts):
1. /home/user/downloads/climate_temp_v1.txt  
   Content exactly:  
        date,temp
        2024-01-01,23
        2024-01-02,22

2. /home/user/downloads/climate_temp_v2.txt  
   Content exactly:  
        date,temp
        2024-01-01,24
        2024-01-02,23
        2024-01-03,25

3. /home/user/downloads/climate_precip_v1.txt  
   Content exactly:  
        date,precip_mm
        2024-01-01,5
        2024-01-02,0

4. /home/user/downloads/readme.txt  
   Content: “Placeholder README – do not move.”

Expected final state after agent’s work:
Directories:
- /home/user/research/datasets/climate/raw/
- /home/user/research/datasets/climate/doc/

Files with contents (bit-identical to initial .txt files):
- /home/user/research/datasets/climate/raw/climate_temp_v1.csv   (same three-line content)
- /home/user/research/datasets/climate/raw/climate_temp_v2.csv   (same four-line content)
- /home/user/research/datasets/climate/raw/climate_precip_v1.csv (same three-line content)

Symbolic link:
- /home/user/research/datasets/climate/latest.csv → raw/climate_temp_v2.csv

Summary file:
- /home/user/research/datasets/climate/summary.txt  
  Exact allowed set of lines (order doesn’t matter, but each must appear once):  
     climate_temp_v1.csv,3  
     climate_temp_v2.csv,4  
     climate_precip_v1.csv,3  

Operations log (exact text, six lines, each terminated with Unix LF):
1. Created directory tree
2. Moved and renamed files
3. Verified file contents
4. Created symbolic link
5. Generated summary.txt
6. Cleanup complete
